intent: Stop hosts/install.sh from chattr +i'ing its own git-tracked source and generate_hosts_file.sh. Immutable tracked files break git tooling: the pre-push ci-mirror gate runs `pre-commit run --all-files`, whose end-of-file-fixer opens every file rb+ and dies with 'PermissionError: [Errno 1] Operation not permitted', so no push from a normal checkout can succeed (every push had to be routed through a throwaway git worktree, where a fresh checkout has new, non-immutable inodes). The same immutability also breaks pre-commit's unstaged-changes stash and silently reverts unrelated edits.
scope: Cleared the immutable bit on hosts/install.sh and hosts/generate_hosts_file.sh so no tracked repo file is immutable
Non-goals: any change to hosts blocklist content, generation logic, or the runtime guard stack
changes: Removed the `chattr +i` self-lock of install.sh and generate_hosts_file.sh, replaced with a comment explaining why it must not be reintroduced.

Runtime hosts enforcement is unaffected and independent: /etc/hosts is itself chattr +i, hosts-guard.path watches and re-enforces it via enforce-hosts.sh against the /usr/local/share/locked-hosts canonical snapshot, and hosts-bind-mount.service pins it. Editing the sources changes nothing until install.sh is re-run as root, which regenerates the guarded artifacts.
Companion to the same fix in setup_midnight_shutdown.sh (commit 6e3d61d).

verification.command: bash -n + shellcheck --severity=warning on hosts/install.sh; git ls-files | xargs lsattr; git push origin main from the normal checkout
verification.evidence: parse OK; shellcheck CLEAN; no removed vars (INSTALL_SCRIPT/GEN_SCRIPT/SCRIPT_DIR) referenced later; no tracked repo file reports the immutable bit; push from main succeeds without a worktree and pre-commit logs 'Restored changes from ...' (the restore step that previously never ran).

risks: Removes one speed bump: the hosts source scripts can now be edited without first running `sudo chattr -i`. The guarded /etc/hosts, the path watcher, the canonical snapshot and the bind mount remain the substantive controls.
rollback: git revert the commit and re-run `sudo bash linux_configuration/scripts/periodic_background/hosts/install.sh` to restore the self-locks.
Validate after rollback: lsattr shows the 'i' bit on both scripts; note that pushes from main will again fail the ci-mirror gate and require a worktree.
